commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Woe to Ariel, to Ariel, the city where David dwelt. add year to year; let feasts run their cycle. This word signifies the lion of God, and here is taken for the strong city of Jerusalem. (Challoner) It was destroyed by the Chaldeans, (4 Kings xxv. ) and still more by the Romans, 40 years after. (Calmet) (Worthington) Ezechiel (xliii. ) styles the altar of holocausts Ariel. The Hebrew means also "inhabited. "(Haydock) Sion was called the city of David. (Calmet) The invasion (Haydock) of Sennacherib is here foretold (Forcr. ) two years before, chap.

commentaries.author: Jerome
commentaries.content: [Zion] formed the city that David formerly stormed and afterwards rebuilt. Of its storming it is written, “Woe to Ariel, to Ariel”—that is, God’s lion (and indeed in those days it was extremely strong). - "Letter 108.9"

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
